tape: Cooler-than-expected June CPI (3.5% vs 3.8% expected) pulled VIX down to ~15.3 from 17.1, but the reinstated Strait of Hormuz blockade/20% cargo toll (effective 4pm ET today) keeps futures capped/mixed as Middle East escalation offsets the inflation relief; book is 100% cash (all prior positions were closed outside the bot earlier today per broker reconciliation), so no held-position risk right now.

alerts.headline: Extremely volatile microcap: multiple sources show BJDX up sharply today (estimates range 43%-64% depending on source/timestamp) off a low-$1 base, but no fresh confirmable catalyst found beyond the known SYMON-II trial progress and 7/7 cash-runway headline already in risk.json. This ticker carries the risk officer's CRITICAL going-concern/Chapter 7-disclosure and warrant-overhang flag - treat any price pop as noise/illiquidity, not signal.
No new news since prior snapshot; $1B+ Equinor backlog and Chad Deaton's 7/2 insider buy remain the latest catalysts. Stock down from 52-week high amid oil-driven volatility.
No new news; DLA bridge-mod contract extension to 11/29/26 remains the latest catalyst. Earnings scheduled 7/29.
No new news; Morgan Stanley/H.C. Wainwright PT hikes (6/25) and the 6/24 $100M offering remain the latest known catalysts.
No new news; rolling BLA submission progress and Feb-2026 $100M underwritten offering (now underwater near $3.75-3.83) remain unchanged.
No new news on the Serapha Bio reverse-merger (still expected to close Q4 2026, pre-merger holders ~3.7% of combined entity); unchanged from prior snapshot.
No new news; Q1 miss (-$0.20 EPS vs -$0.12 est) and Jefferies' PT cut to $22 (from $22.50, still Buy) are the latest known items. Earnings 8/10.

movers.note: Large reported intraday swing (source estimates vary widely, 43-64%) on a sub-$2 going-concern microcap - illiquid/volatile, not a clean signal.
